published_prior_art.cite: DoudchenkoImbens2016: signed/unrestricted synthetic-control weights, but not the sharp bounded-error support interval
NazaretShiBlei2024: misspecification bounds/intervals for linear SC assumptions, not this oracle bridge support-function interval
CattaneoFengPalombaTitiunik2025JSS: scpi prediction-interval workflow, no deterministic l1-leverage partial-ID radius report
KelloggMogstadPouliotTorgovitsky2021 and CattaneoFengPalombaTitiunik2025JSS: extrapolation-bias/model-reporting comparators, not open-class equal-fit unequal-radius separation

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposal advertises scpi OLS/lasso/ridge/L1-L2 workflows, but the locked weight class requires affine sum-one weights; scpi's OLS/ridge/lasso constraints can be unconstrained or norm-constrained rather than affine, so either broaden W to finite weights or restrict the claimed workflow coverage.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The flagship open-class claim quantifies over 'two workflows' in A_sc without fixing the actual objective, tuning rule, and local stability topology; name the pair, e.g. simplex SCM versus fixed-lambda ridge/OLS, so the open-set statement is mathematically determinate.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary scalar-bookkeeping
soundness_flags.one_line: The claim that different leverage products imply different sharp radii fails when eta=0; add eta>0 to the corollary.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch the locked convention and Theorem 3 so the scpi comparator is exactly covered: either allow all finite donor-weight vectors or state that only affine-normalized scpi outputs are in scope.
Make Conjecture 1 name one concrete workflow pair and fixed tuning regime, then state openness relative to that pair's data-to-weight map.
Add an explicit comparison to Nazaret-Shi-Blei's M/James misspecification intervals explaining why their simplex/Wasserstein bounds do not subsume the signed-weight oracle bridge radius.

recommended_next_step: Revise the workflow/weight-class convention and add eta>0 to the scalar corollary; the novelty kernel can survive these local patches.
